description: Diameter bands, DDR→drill cycle, stock-to-leave, RF/FINISH split, and tap drill table — mirrors 4. process_selection.py until a loader is wired.

preferred_machine.notes: Boss and large_bore use this for primary vs alternate sequences; holes and planar_face always milling.

drill_diameter_bands_mm.twist_band_rule: micro_drill_max_exclusive ≤ d ≤ twist_drill_max_inclusive → spot_drill then twist_drill
drill_diameter_bands_mm.core_band_rule: twist_drill_max_inclusive < d ≤ core_drill_max_inclusive → spot_drill, pilot_drill (60% of final D), core_drill

rules: ddr is None or ddr ≤ ddr_standard_max_inclusive → standard

boss_turning_note: Below this diameter, milling contour is preferred when PREFERRED_MACHINE is milling.

face_mill_max_ap_mm.rule: If feature depth > max_ap for material, emit RF then FINISH face_mill passes.

face_mill_subtypes.description: Subtypes for planar_face features, classified by surface context. Source: ShiaanX_Strategy_Rules_Final for face.xlsx (Gaurav, 2024-05-01).

subtypes.full_surface: Unobstructed flat surface — no bosses, holes, or slots breaking the face. Entry from outside the part boundary.
subtypes.boss_surface: Flat face sitting on top of a boss cluster — adjacent to boss walls on one or more sides. Use end mill rather than face mill for narrow widths.
subtypes.interrupted: Face broken by holes, slots, or pockets — tool crosses interrupted material. Use helical entry to avoid impact on re-entry.

detection_rules.boss_surface: cluster is adjacent (shares edge) to one or more boss-type clusters
detection_rules.interrupted: cluster has one or more hole/pocket/slot child features breaking the face plane
detection_rules.full_surface: neither boss_surface nor interrupted conditions met

face_mill_width_bands_mm.description: Width-based tool selection for face features. Width = largest bounding box extent in the machined plane. Source: strategy rules sheet (Gaurav).

bands.note: Narrow face — end mill preferred, face mill too wide
Large face — switch to face mill cutter
Very large face — 50mm face mill, may require multiple passes

face_mill_stock_to_leave_z_mm.description: Z-axis stock to leave after face_mill RF pass (before FINISH). Different from general MATERIAL_STOCK_TABLE — facing is axial so XY stock is irrelevant. Source: strategy rules sheet (Gaurav, R-001 Notes).

tap_drill_table_mm.comment: Key = nominal metric thread diameter; value = ISO 68-1 tap drill diameter. Non-table: nearest within 0.5mm else thread_dia * 0.8

warnings: Slot/pocket process branches may still fall through to manual_review in select_process() until those feature types are fully wired.

LIVE (applied at import): all drill bands, DDR thresholds, boss_turning_min_diameter, stock_to_leave tables, face_mill_max_ap, rf_split_operations, drill_like_operations, tap_drill_table, corner_r_feature_types.
